prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde (Boletim Hepatites Virais) / WHO
prevalence.trend: stable to decreasing with vaccination; higher prevalence in some Amazon/indigenous settings

incidence.source: Ministério da Saúde viral hepatitis notifications

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: HBsAg positive for chronic infection (≥6 months), HBV DNA quantification and ALT for staging; per national hepatitis guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: antenatal HBsAg testing common in SUS prenatal care; broader screening targeted to risk groups
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; treatment coverage limited by diagnosis gaps and linkage-to-care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Prenatal screening and confirmatory serology/viral load in SUS; fibrosis staging via labs/imaging with referral pathways
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Tenofovir or entecavir for eligible chronic HBV; neonatal immunoprophylaxis (vaccine + HBIG where indicated) and universal vaccination via PNI
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis in adults, linkage-to-care gaps, and higher-burden pockets in remote regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong national immunization program and SUS access to first-line antivirals for eligible patients
